content:
1 min read
Just now

--

Sony is planning to integrate smart tech into their newer game development approaches. They’ve also claimed that automation won’t just be a minor addition, but will actually act as a core part of future game development at PlayStation. It’s honestly no surprise that Sony is stepping into this. Maybe they’ll even drop a new signature gaming device that provides a way better cost to specs ratio than whatever we’ve been seeing so far.
Although no major leaks or anything have come up yet, it’s obvious a tech giant like Sony is working on smaller pieces like implementing new algorithms in 3D modeling, resolution, and performance measures. Even though people want the Sony PlayStation to remain a standalone gaming console, I hope there is no need for external hardware just to play our favorite games. Because if that happens, Sony will lose a lot of consumers, especially the ones who are completely against the extensive use of automated systems. A little bit of background tech is fine, but making it the core component might honestly bug some people off.
